layout.container: flex flex-col lg:flex-row h-screen w-full overflow-hidden bg-white text-[#0A0A0A]
layout.left_pane: w-full lg:w-5/12 h-full overflow-y-auto border-r border-border bg-[#F8F9FA] p-6 lg:p-12
layout.right_pane: w-full lg:w-7/12 h-full flex flex-col overflow-y-auto bg-white p-6 lg:p-12

components.buttons: Flat, sharp edges, high contrast. Primary: bg-[#002FA7] text-white hover:bg-[#0A0A0A]. Secondary: border border-[#0A0A0A] text-[#0A0A0A] hover:bg-[#F8F9FA]
components.inputs: Shadcn Textarea and Input, sharp edges, focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#002FA7] focus:border-transparent
components.accordion: For documentation viewer. Clean lines, border-b only, bold titles. Use Shadcn Accordion.
components.code_block: Terminal look. bg-[#0A0A0A] text-white p-6 font-mono text-sm overflow-x-auto relative. Must include 'Copy' button in top right.
components.badges: For example prompts. border border-border text-xs uppercase tracking-wider bg-white hover:bg-[#F8F9FA] cursor-pointer rounded-none px-3 py-1

motion.hover: Subtle background color shifts, crisp and immediate. No bouncy animations.
motion.loading: Typewriter effect for code generation, or a brutalist indeterminate progress bar.

hero_background.description: Minimalist architectural representation of AI. Use as the cover image in the documentation section (Left Pane) or a welcome card.

abstract_matrix.description: Blue glowing digital lines. Can be used as a subtle overlay texture in the code generator empty state.

specific_instructions_for_main_agent: Build a dual-pane hybrid application layout: Left pane for the Academic Project Documentation, Right pane for the LLM Code Generator workspace.

In the Left Pane, generate and display all required academic sections: Abstract, Introduction, Objectives, Literature Review, Methodology, System Architecture, Tools and Technologies, Implementation, Advantages and Limitations, Future Scope, and Conclusion. Use a Shadcn Accordion or structured article layout for readability.
Add a 'Download Documentation' button (e.g., simulated PDF download or print trigger) in the documentation pane header.
In the Right Pane, build the interactive Code Generation interface.
Include a 'Programming Language Selector' (e.g., Python, JavaScript) using Shadcn Select.
Include a Natural Language Input Field (Textarea) and an 'Example Prompts Gallery' (clickable badges) below it to auto-fill the textarea.
Implement a mock OpenAI service (or use a real API if key is provided) to simulate natural language to code generation with a realistic processing delay.
Render the generated code using 'react-syntax-highlighter' or a custom pre/code block with a dark theme (Terminal look) to contrast the light UI.
Include a 'Copy Generated Code' button inside the code display.
Below the code output, render a 'Code Explanation Section' simulating the LLM's explanation of the logic, with comments referenced.
Ensure the design strictly follows 'Swiss & High-Contrast' principles (Archetype 4): sharp edges, 1px borders, high contrast, minimal unnecessary shadows. Use #002FA7 for the primary action button.
Install and use Google Fonts: Chivo (headings), IBM Plex Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (code).
CRITICAL: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='generate-btn').
Use Phosphor Icons or lucide-react for interface icons.
Ensure the application is desktop-optimized but responsive (stack panes on mobile).

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text against #F8F9FA or #FFFFFF backgrounds has a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1.
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have a visible focus ring (e.g., focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#002FA7] focus:outline-none).
